headline: Super Micro Computer Hit With Securities Fraud Lawsuit Amid Alleged China Sales Cover-Up And Nvidia Chip Smuggling Scandal
summary: Super Micro Computer is facing a shareholder lawsuit alleging it misled investors by concealing China-linked sales and compliance risks tied to Nvidia chips, after criminal charges against insiders triggered a sharp stock plunge.

headline: Why Super Micro Computer (SMCI) Is Down 29.5% After Indictment Over Alleged China AI Server Exports And What's Next
summary: In March 2026, U.S. prosecutors indicted three people connected to Super Micro Computer, including co-founder Yih-Shyan “Wally” Liaw, over alleged illegal exports of Nvidia-powered AI servers to China, prompting his resignation from the board and the appointment of DeAnna Luna as acting Chief Compliance Officer. At the same time, Super Micro has been rolling out an expanded portfolio of Nvidia Blackwell- and Vera Rubin-based AI systems and deepening ecosystem partnerships, highlighting a...

headline: RAMageddon Arrives: AI’s Endless Appetite Just Killed the PC Comeback
summary: The global PC market has clawed its way back from a brutal slump in 2022 and 2023, when shipments cratered amid post-pandemic oversupply, with 2024 delivering modest growth as businesses and consumers began refreshing aging fleets. By 2025, the rebound appeared to be accelerating sharply — shipments rose 9.3% year-over-year, fueled by commercial upgrades to ... headline: Arm to Sell Its Own Chips, Eyeing Sales Goal of $15 Billion
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Arm Holdings Plc, which made its name licensing technology to semiconductor makers, will begin selling its own chips for the first time, adding a business that it expects to generate about $15 billion annually within five years. Meta Platforms Inc. will be the first major customer for the company’s chip, called an AGI CPU, Arm said Tuesday at an event in San Francisco. The product will have as many as 136 cores — a measure of processing power — and draw 300 watts of electricity, A

headline: Super Micro Deepens Mirantis Ties To Standardize Open Source AI Clouds
summary: Super Micro Computer (NasdaqGS:SMCI) is expanding its ecosystem partnerships to automate AI cloud infrastructure with Mirantis. The collaboration focuses on integrating open source platforms, including k0rdent AI, with NVIDIA based GPU cloud environments. The move targets sovereign AI and hybrid GPU cloud use cases, with an emphasis on operational automation and reduced manual provisioning.
